text:
A commentator at theOlympicswho was sacked for sexist remarks aboutAustralia’s women swimmers has apologised ‘for causing offence’.

Eurosport commentator Bob Ballard came under fire onsocial mediaafter saying women like ‘hanging around doing their make-up’.

Ballard made the comment shortly after Australia quartet Emma McKeon, Shayna Jack, Mollie O’Callaghan and Meg Harris secured 4x100m freestyle relay gold in the pool.

‘Well, the women just finishing up,’ said Ballard, who has previously covered sports events for BBC Radio 5 Live and talkSPORT.

‘You know what women are like… hanging around, doing their make-up.’

Eurosport co-commentator Lizzie Simmonds described the comment as ‘outrageous’, a comment would drew a chuckle from Ballard. The TV broadcaster promptly removed Ballard from further gigs in Paris.

‘During a segment of Eurosport’s coverage last night, commentator Bob Ballard made an inappropriate comment,’ a statement read.

‘To that end, he has been removed from our commentary roster with immediate effect.’

Breaking his silence, Ballard has said it was ‘never my intention to upset or belittle anyone’, also urging people not to ‘pile on’ his former co-presenter Simmonds.

‘The comments I made during the Australian freestyle relay victory ceremony on Saturday have caused some offence,’ Ballard tweeted on Monday.

‘It was never my intention to upset or belittle anyone and, if I did, I apologise. I am a massive advocate of women’s sport.

Right here it is the statement pt 1:The comments I made during the Australian freestyle relay victory ceremony on Saturday have caused some offence. It was never my intention to upset or belittle anyone and, if I did, I apologise. I am a massive advocate of womenâs sport.I

‘I shall miss the Eurosport team, dearly and wish them all the best for the rest of the Olympics.

‘P.s. Don’t pile in on @LizzieSimmonds1 – please! She has nothing to do with this.’

At theParis opening ceremony, International Olympic Committee (IOC) President Thomas Bach hailed the 2024 Games as the most ‘inclusive’ of its kind.

‘All of us will experience Olympic Games that are more inclusive, more urban, younger and more sustainable,’ he said. ‘The first Olympic Games with full gender parity on the field of play.’

Day two about to rock and roll in the swimming in Paris – every race live on@discoveryplusUKand the best action on@eurosportwith@LizzieSimmonds1and my âgoodâ self.pic.twitter.com/UUsUACEbIu

In winning freestyle relay gold, Australia star McKeon bagged her 12th Olympic medal and became the nation’s most successful swimmer, surpassing Ian Thorpe with six golds.

‘Wow. I don’t keep track of that kind of stuff. I hear it from you guys. It’s an honour really,’ she toldFoxSportsafter being told of the milestone.

‘To be part of this (4x100m relay team), it’s extremely hard and this is a team of six with Bronte (Campbell) and Liv (Olivia Wunsch, heat swimmers) – we couldn’t do it without this group of six.

‘Everyone wants to be part of this team. I feel really honoured to be part of it.

‘It’s definitely been a rough couple of years, especially this year. I definitely had my doubts. I just really wanted to be part of this team tonight.’

Thorpe, meanwhile, sent a classy message to McKeon after being displaced as Australia’s most successful swimmer.

‘That was how I felt – we were equal on gold and she had one more silver or something like that,’ he said. ‘So I counted it the normal way so I thought she was already there.

‘I couldn’t think of anyone better to have that title. But it may be a title only for now… there’s some young swimmers that are swimming exceptionally well on our swim team.’

title: Olympics commentator sacked for sexist remarks issues statement explaining comments | Metro News
description: Bob Ballard was sacked from Olympics duty 'with immediate effect' after making a sexist remark about Australia's women swimmers.
